creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
From <https://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-questions/2021-July/294474.html>: 

> … IPv6 connectivity to
> 
> Name:   update2.freebsd.org
> Address: 2620:11c:5001:1099:1337::117
> 
> appears to have stopped working.
> 
> Long delays running freebsd-update and does not respond to ping6 either. 
> IPv6 on update.freebsd.org appears to be working normally. Tested from 2 
> separate and dissimilar networks.

Here: 

% ping -6 update.freebsd.org
PING6(56=40+8+8 bytes) 2001:…:…:…::2 --> 2001:4978:1000::2319:16
16 bytes from 2001:4978:1000::2319:16, icmp_seq=0 hlim=57 time=95.715 ms
16 bytes from 2001:4978:1000::2319:16, icmp_seq=1 hlim=57 time=89.548 ms
16 bytes from 2001:4978:1000::2319:16, icmp_seq=2 hlim=57 time=100.099 ms
16 bytes from 2001:4978:1000::2319:16, icmp_seq=3 hlim=57 time=90.091 ms
16 bytes from 2001:4978:1000::2319:16, icmp_seq=4 hlim=57 time=89.682 ms
^C
--- update.freebsd.org ping6 statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/std-dev = 89.548/93.027/100.099/4.223 ms
% ping -6 update2.freebsd.org
PING6(56=40+8+8 bytes) 2001:…:…:…::2 --> 2620:11c:5001:1099:1337::117
^C
--- update2.freebsd.org ping6 statistics ---
16 packets transmitted, 0 packets received, 100.0% packet loss
% ping -4 update2.freebsd.org
PING update2.freebsd.org (204.15.11.69): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 204.15.11.69: icmp_seq=0 ttl=50 time=134.442 ms
64 bytes from 204.15.11.69: icmp_seq=1 ttl=50 time=134.461 ms
64 bytes from 204.15.11.69: icmp_seq=2 ttl=50 time=134.205 ms
64 bytes from 204.15.11.69: icmp_seq=3 ttl=50 time=134.448 ms
64 bytes from 204.15.11.69: icmp_seq=4 ttl=50 time=134.098 ms
^C
--- update2.freebsd.org ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/stddev = 134.098/134.331/134.461/0.150 ms
% date ; uname -KrU
Fri 30 Jul 2021 08:05:52 BST
14.0-CURRENT 1400026 1400026
%
